characters.name: Andrew
characters.description: Andrew DavisBirthdayLate 1950s to Early 1960sFromUnknownEye ColorBrownHair ColorBrownOccupationUnknownRelationshipsFriendsFezSteven HydeDonna PinciottiSeriesPortrayed byJustin LongSeason(s)8Only seen"Love of My Life"Andrew DavisisFez's friend back from his home country. He has a very different accent than Fez, and a different skin tone, which the two of them explain is due to the fact that they live on two different sides of the island back home. Andrew convinces Fez to come back to their home country after he was rejected byJackie Burkhart. When he informed Jackie that Fez was leaving, and could not stay knowing that he hurt the woman he loved, Jackie was convinced that she had to win Fez back. Andrew seems rather disdainful of Jackie, insulting both her andDonna(who he had mistaken for Jackie). Still, like Fez, he has a fondness for women, and flirted with both Donna, and Jackie, who he offered to make out with.

She is portrayed byJessica Simpson. Annette is short (5’4”) and she is Kelso's girlfriend from California. Eric and Donna once referred to her as a blonde version of Jackie, or "Blackie." Despite this, Jackie and Annette hated each other. Once at The Hub, Jackie said to Annette "I didn't know they let 'slutballs' in here". Annette said since she saw Jackie in there she thought it was okay. She was Michael Kelso's girlfriend for a brief period of time in Season 5. During the premiere episode of Season 5, Annette and Kelso decide to spend their last day together doing fun things rather than have sex much to Kelso's disliking. She is dim-witted like Kelso. characters.name: Bea Sigurdson
characters.description: Beatrice SigurdsonBornBeatrice Dorothy HeprageBirthdayMay 23, 1910Nickname(s)Bea (by everyone)Mom, Frigid Witch (byKitty)Grandma (byLaurieandEric)Nutbag (byDonna)Eye ColorBlueHair ColorWhiteDied1980sRelationshipsFamilyBurt Sigurdson† (husband)Kitty Forman(daughter)Paula Sigurdson(daughter)Red Forman(son-in-law)Laurie Forman(granddaughter)Eric Forman(grandson)Penny Sigurdson(granddaughter)Leia Forman(great-granddaughter)Donna Pinciotti(granddaughter-in-law)SeriesPortrayed byBetty WhiteSeason(s)5(That '70s Show)First seen"Heartbreaker"Last seen"Babe I'm Gonna Leave You" Beatrice "Bea" Dorothy Sigurdsonwas a fictional character onFOXcomedyThat '70s Show. She was the mother ofKitty Formanand was portrayed by the lateBetty White. Bea is 5’5”. She is looked like a friendly old lady, but was actually very loud and very rude, even to her own family. She appeared in a few episodes inSeason 5. She was often seen yelling and nagging a lot at her husband, Burt or said something very rude to Kitty in a sunny demeanor. InYour Time Is Gonna Comeafter Burt bought an ostrich farm in which all the ostriches would be eaten by the coyotes due to the ostriches being right next to the coyote farm the Sigurdsons became bankrupt and were forced to sell their home. After they arrived at the Forman house and explained the situation to Red and Kitty, they then took them in despite Red's dismay. While Kitty was sympathetic to her father, Bea however blamed Burt for losing all their money. Later on, when Burt collapsed in the driveway, the Formans, Hyde, Kelso, Fez and Bea took Burt to the hospital where he dies. Despite becoming a widow, she showed little emotion after her husband's death, caused Kitty to be angry by this. Kitty did catch her crying in a bedroom later, proving that despite her constant yelling and nagging at Burt, she did however love her husband. This also caused Kitty to see her mother does have empathy. When Bea realized Kitty saw, the left her mother to her privacy to grieve. Bea is 5’5”. characters.name: Bernice Forman
characters.description: 1970s1950sBernice FormanBornBernice Forman (maiden name unknown)BirthdayJune 23, 1900Nickname(s)Mom (byRedandMarty)Grandma (byEricandLaurie)Mother Forman, Old Lady, Evil Horrible Woman (byKitty)Bitch (by Eric)Grandma Forman (by the Gang)Eye ColorBlueHair ColorWhiteOccupationDancer (formerly)DiedDecember 12, 1976RelationshipsFamilyAlbert Forman† (husband)Red Forman(son)Marty Forman(son)Jerry Forman(son)Kitty Forman(daughter-in-law)Laurie Forman(granddaughter)Eric Forman(grandson)Leia Forman(great-granddaughter)Donna Pinciotti(granddaughter-in-law)FriendsFez(friend)SeriesPortrayed byMarion RossSeason(s)1 • 2 (That '70s Show)First seen"Sunday, Bloody Sunday"Last seen"Halloween" (Flashback)Episodes4 Bernice Formanis a recurring character on the FOX comedyThat '70s Show. She is portrayed byMarion Ross. She is 5’6” and it is unknown when she was born, but since Red was 49 at the beginning of the show (1976) she was probably born between 1897-1902. She only appeared in three episodes in the first season, and during all those episodes it is shown that she has a difficult relationship withKitty, constantly criticizing her. Her dislike of Kitty stems from the fact that Red was dating a very wealthy woman, but he married Kitty instead. She died whileEricwas driving her to the airport. His last words to her were: "I don't think being nice for a whole day would kill you." and then she dropped dead on Eric's shoulder. Eric feels incredibly guilty about this but at the end of the episode he works it out.

characters.description: 1990s1970sBetsy KelsoBornElizabeth Victoria KelsoBirthdayJanuary 15, 1979Nickname(s)Betsy (by everyone)My Baby Girl, My Daughter, Bambi (byKelso)Feeble-minded bun-in-her-oven (byHyde)That thing (byEric)Fat girl (byJackie)Babe (byNate)FromPoint Place, Wisconsin (birthplace)ResidenceSalamanca, Spain (formerly)Chicago, Illinois (formerly)Eye ColorBrownHair ColorBrownOccupationStudentRelationshipsFamilyMichael Kelso(father)Brooke Rockwell(mother)Jay Kelso(younger paternal half-brother)John Kelso(paternal grandfather)Mrs. Kelso(paternal grandmother)Casey Kelso(paternal uncle)Unnamed paternal unclesUnnamed paternal auntBetsy(great-grandmother)Carolyn Rockwell(maternal grandmother)Jackie Burkhart(godmother/stepmother)Steven Hyde(godfather)Jack Burkhart(step-grandfather)Pam Burkhart(step-grandmother)RomancesNate Runck(ex-boyfriend)SeriesPortrayed byKira Kosarin(That '90s Show)Season(s)7(That '70s Show)2(That '90s Show)First seenOh, Baby (We Got a Good Thing Goin')Last seenLife Is a HighwayElizabeth "Betsy" Victoria Kelsois a character on FOX comedyThat '70s ShowandThat '90s Show. She is the daughter ofMichael KelsoandBrooke Rockwell, the goddaughter, later stepdaughter ofJackie Burkhartand the older half-sister ofJay Kelso. She is 5’8 ½”. My Baby Girl, My Daughter, Bambi (byKelso)Feeble-minded bun-in-her-oven (byHyde)That thing (byEric)Fat girl (byJackie)Babe (byNate) 2(That '90s Show) Betsy is the daughter ofMichael KelsoandBrooke Rockwell. She was conceived during a Molly Hatchet concert. ThroughoutSeason 6Kelso must adjust to life as an expecting father. Betsy is born at the beginning ofSeason 7. She lives with her mother and grandmother in Chicago. After her birth, Kelso watched her for the day but Betsy wouldn't stop crying and he began to doubt himself as a father because he was barely in her life when she was born. At the end of the episode, Betsy was held by her father and finally stopped crying as Kelso realized that she finally realized who he was. Kelso also promised Brooke to be more around because he needed to be a part of his daughters life and this was agreed on. Kelso acted on his promise and spent every weekend for the past month with his daughter it was even allowed to pick her godparents. At the beginning ofSeason 8, Kelso moves to Chicago to be closer to his daughter. In the '90s, it is unknown what her relationship is like with her father; since he moved back to Point Place in the early '80s but it can be assumed that she was mainly raised by her mother later in the '80s; with her father somewhat involved.

Dumbass, Cry Baby (byRed)Bob the Stress Causer (byKitty)Bobby (byPam)Barry (mistakenly byPatty)Grandpa Bob (byLeia) 1•2(That '90s Show) Bob Pinciotti is a 5’11” Italian-American man with dark curly brown hair and dark brown eyes. The husband ofMidge Pinciottiand father ofValerie,Tina, andDonna Pinciotti. Bob was born in 1929 in Madison, Wisconsin where he met Midge and they have three children. Though after "Eric's Burger Job" when Tina appeared briefly, she was never seen again. She was later mentioned in a "Young and the Restless" parody in "Vanstock" where the narrator said "And what happened to Midge's daughter Tina?" She was never mentioned or seen again. Valerie was mentioned in the episode "Eric's Birthday" when Midge gives Kitty a book of poems as how she coped "...when Valerie went to school". After "Vanstock", no other child other than Donna had been mentioned or seen, making Donna an only child. Some believe that Tina had ran away do to anxiety issues and not being as loved as Donna. Bob is very dim-witted. Though Eric and Donna always say they have known each other forever, in "Class Picture" show them meeting when they are older. The flashback shows that Bob and Midge moved right next door toRedandKitty Formanand their sonEricin 1968 when both Donna and Eric were 7. Bob and Donna introduced themselves without Midge as she had her finger caught in something (Tanya Roberts was not on the show anymore). Their address was revealed to be residing at 853, and the street they live on is Marie Dr. Their house is apparently only about 20 steps away from The Forman's and it is filled with color and delight. Eric once described the Pinciotti livingroom as a "turquoise and chrome, disaster of a room". Donna believed it was classy. Bob and Midge got married while still in high schoo

characters.name: Bray
characters.description: BrayBornBray (first name unknown)Birthday1930sNickname(s)Mr. Bray (by everyone)ResidencePoint Place, WisconsinEye ColorBlueHair ColorLight Brown (graying, balding)OccupationGuidance Counselor at thePoint Place High SchoolRelationshipsRomancesDonna Pinciotti(crush)SeriesPortrayed byChris ElliotSeason(s)7(That '70s Show)Only seen2000 Light Years From HomeMr. Brayis a character that was introduced in the episode2000 Light Years From Home. He is portrayed by Chris Elliot. Mr. Bray was first introduced whenEricandDonnacame to see him in hopes of helping Eric get better opportunities to allow him get the money that he needs to go to college with Bray super happy to see Donna (whom he has an inappropriate crush on her; with her fully aware of it) and doesn’t really give any respect for Eric but agrees to talk to them about Eric’s situation. Later on Eric tells his family, friends and Donna that Bray has agreed to help him out by getting him into a program that lets people teach impoverished areas for a year and that it will pay for college with Eric signing up for the program and that he will be going to Africa; withRedhappy by the news and Donna andKittyextremely upset about Eric’s decision. Later on in the episode Bray visits Eric in his home to give him a brochure with some survival tips on Africa and introducing himself to Red and Kitty with Red suspicious of him due to seeing him always driving by the neighborhood (unaware that Bray indeed does this to spy on Donna at her house). Bray then asks Eric of how Donna is taking the news (with him hoping Donna broke up with him and that Bray would have her all for himself) but Eric then leaves to go talk to Donna. In the kitchen Bray joins Red and Kitty at the table and eats their food while Bray asks them if they have adaughter, leaving Red and Kitty creeped out and silent. characters.name: Bud Hyde
characters.description: Bud HydeBornSteven James HydeBirthdayJanuary 17, 1939Nickname(s)Bud (preferred nickname)Dad (byHyde)Dirtbag, Father of the Year (byEric)Mr. Hyde's Dad (byFez)Bad Parent (byKitty)Eye ColorBlueHair ColorDark BrownOccupationBartenderRelationshipsFamilySteven Hyde(step-son)Hyde's uncle (brother or ex-brother-in-law)RomancesEdna Hyde(ex-wife/girlfriend)SeriesPortrayed byRobert HaysSeason(s)3(That '70s Show)First seen"Hyde's Father"Last seen"Hyde's Christmas Rager"Episodes2 Steven "Bud" James Hyde Sr.is a character on FOX comedyThat '70s Show. He is portrayed byRobert Hays. Tall (6’1½”) and cool European-American man with long brown hair and dark brown eyes. Bud isSteven Hyde's absentee step-father and ex-husband ofEdna Hyde. Bud Hyde worked as a bartender and is an alcoholic. Until the end of the 6th season, Steven Hyde thought Bud was his biological father. Bud Hyde abandoned Steven when he was a child and Steven was always angry at Bud for this. They temporarily reconciled in season 3, with Hyde even moving in with him. However, he would leave again at the beginning of season 4 after getting back together with Edna – forcing Hyde to move back to the Forman house. Hyde mentioned that Bud did attempt to get in touch with him again. However, Bud was passed out drunk on Hyde's car. He is portrayed byMitch Pileggi. After Second World War the two came back to the country and shortly after that lost touch, Bull moved to Chicago and started having affairs with multiple women. He got married withJoy, became rich and eventually came back toPoint Place, meeting Red again. They started hanging out again but with time Red understood that Bull is not the same person that he used to be; Bull's in an open relationship with his wife and casually hooks up with other women. This causes Red to sever ties with Bull permanently.

characters.name: Carol
characters.description: CarolBornCarol (last name unknown)Birthday1930sResidencePoint Place, WisconsinEye ColorBlueHair ColorBlondeOccupationUnknownRelationshipsRomancesBob Pinciotti(blind date)SeriesPortrayed byJenilee HarrisonSeason(s)2(That '70s Show)Only seenRed's BirthdayCarolis a character that was introduced in the episodeRed's Birthday. She is portrayed byJenilee Harrison. Carol was first introduced when Bob Pinciotti brought her as his blind date, along with Midge bringing Ted as her date as well (due to Bob and Midge separating but are both seeing other people while they are still living together) when the four people joined Red and Kitty for Red’s birthday celebration, much to his chagrin. After Bob and Midge explain the couple of each meeting their dates Red at first wanted to leave but changes his mind when a waiter arrives with all of their served food, including Red getting his T-bone steak. Red then announces to everyone that before he eats his steak he wants to make a birthday wish with him saying "I wish... everyone would shut up.", with Kitty silently acknowledging this and leaving the other couples speechless. Later on in the evening Ted asks Midge if she read the Equus and inadvertently insults her; Bob then confronts him that if he insults his wife one more time Ted is going to be wearing his "steak as a hat". This then pleases Midge and both the Pinciottis start to reminisce the good old days in their relationship before they both openly kiss each others hands in front of Red, Kitty and their dates before Bob excuses himself to find his wallet, Midge then excuses herself to find her "sex with Bob in the car", leaving Red and Kitty speechless. Not wanting to be here any longer, Red then asks the waiter for the check, which he does and instead of paying for the entire meal himself Red gives Ted the check to pay for before he and Kitty leave the restaurant, leaving only Carol and Ted. Red then announces to everyone that before he eats his steak he wants to make a birthday wish with him saying "I wish... everyone would shut up.", with Kitty silently acknowledging this and leaving the other couples speechless. Later on in the evening Ted asks Midge if she read the Equus and inadvertently insults her; Bob then confronts him that if he insults his wife one more time Ted is going to be wearing his "steak as a hat". This then pleases Midge and both the Pinciottis start to reminisce the good old days in their relationship before they both openly kiss each others hands in front of Red, Kitty and their dates before Bob excuses himself to find his wallet, Midge then excuses herself to find her "sex with Bob in the car", leaving Red and Kitty speechless. characters.name: Caroline Dupree
characters.description: Caroline DupreeBornCaroline Allison DupreeBirthdayMay 13, 1960Nickname(s)Crazy Caroline (by everyone)Mental Mary, Psycho (byHyde)Eye ColorBlueHair ColorBlondeOccupationStudent at Point Place High School (formerly)HostessRelationshipsRomancesFez(ex-boyfriend)SeriesPortrayed byAllison MunnSeason(s)3•8(That '70s Show)First seen"Fez Gets the Girl"Last seen"Sheer Heart Attack"Episodes8 Mental Mary, Psycho (byHyde) Caroline Allison Dupree, also known asCrazy Carolineis a recurring character on theFOXcomedyThat '70s Showand a recurring antagonist. She is portrayed byAllison Munn. When Caroline is first introduced, she is seen as too nervous to talk to Fez, who she thinks is cute. She is given tickets to a concert by Donna causing her to end up on a date withFez. DuringDine and Dash, Fez tried to impress her while she working as a waitress but she does not have time for him. At the end, she apologizes but realized they are a couple now with their first real fight and tries to reconcile with a kiss. However, he was suffering from her laxative that Donna and Eric had placed in his brownie. After angrily cursing the pair, he dashed past the pleading Caroline while she chased him. InDonna's Panties, she and Fez become boyfriend and girlfriend. During this episode, her true personality is hinted at during their date at the movies. Her true personality is revealed inKitty's Birthday (That's Today?!)when she revealed to Hyde that she thought of choking herself when she thought about him dumping her. InHoly Craps!,she learned that Fez had kissed Jackie and freaked out on Donna and Jackie. She then showed Fez when the girls showed how close they were. Fez then broke up with her. Despite this, she still wanted to be with him as seen inFez Dates Donna, to the point of her stalking him. She ends up spying on his fake dates with Donna, not realizing it was all fake. It truly ends when Donna revealed that Fez didn't like her anymore. She returns, acting just as crazy as before. However Fez still had feelings for her, so he still chose to go out with her. He pretended that Jackie and Donna were maids to get her to calm down once she realized that they weren't alone.

Michigan AveEpisodes6 Casey Kelsois a character on FOX comedyThat '70s Show. He is portrayed byLuke Wilson. Michael Kelso's older brother. He is shorter than Michael (6’1”). While notquiteas dumb as his brother, as he had spent time in the army. Michael Kelso's older brother. He is shorter than Michael (6’1”). While notquiteas dumb as his brother, as he had spent time in the army. However, he is similar to his younger brother in that he is just as a womanizer, senseless and very laid back. The difference between him and his brother is that he completely disregards the harm he inflicts on his lovers, seen when he told Donna that he loved her only to tell Eric he didn't mean it. When breaking up, he admitted to Donna about his lie and was ready to move on entirely. Hyde, Eric, and even Michael joked about him being known as "It's only a misdemeanor!" Casey Kelso, indicating he has gotten into trouble in the past and was something of a loser in Point Place. Donna also noted his arms got bigger post-Army, indicating he wasn't as attractive beforehand. He also has a deep love for his Trans Am, sharing a love of cars like his brother. He is somewhat of a bully, as he repeatedly pulls pranks on his brother and refers to Eric as “Foreplay”. Casey never receives any form of punishment for his actions, save for one instance where he is chased by an angry husband for sleeping with his wife. His first appearance was in Season 4, in the episode entitledDonna Dates a Kelsoand he acts as a love interest for Donna after she and Eric had broken up.
